sentiment.label: risk_off
sentiment.reason_it: Il sentiment di mercato è risk-off a causa della guerra in corso tra USA-Israele e Iran, con il VIX che si attesta a 25.33 (+6.01% rispetto alla precedente chiusura) e il DXY che guadagna lo 0.07% a 99.67. Il prezzo del petrolio (Brent) è sceso del 3.04% a $99.11, mentre l'oro perde il 2.77% a $4423.90/oz. I mercati azionari sono deboli, con il Nikkei che perde l'0.81% e lo Shanghai Composite l'0.89%.
sentiment.reason_en: Market sentiment is risk-off due to the ongoing war between USA-Israel and Iran, with VIX at 25.33 (+6.01%) and DXY up 0.07% to 99.67. Oil prices (Brent) dropped 3.04% to $99.11, while gold lost 2.77% to $4423.90/oz. Equity markets are weak, with Nikkei down 0.81% and Shanghai Composite down 0.89%.

market_impact_summary.it: La guerra in Iran ha causato un aumento del VIX e del DXY, mentre i prezzi del petrolio e dell'oro sono scesi. I mercati azionari asiatici sono stati deboli. Il TLT bond USA 20Y è salito dello 0.96% a 86.84, mentre il rendimento del decennale USA è sceso dell'1.46% a 4.32%.
market_impact_summary.en: The Iran war led to an increase in VIX and DXY, while oil and gold prices declined. Asian equity markets were weak. The TLT bond USA 20Y rose 0.96% to 86.84, while the US 10Y yield fell 1.46% to 4.32%.

audio_script_it:
Benvenuti al vostro Morning Briefing quotidiano. Buongiorno e benvenuti all'aggiornamento finanziario di oggi.

La seduta asiatica ci consegna un Nikkei in calo dello 0.81 percento a 53,315 punti e Shanghai in calo dello 0.89 percento a 3896.82 punti. Questi dati anticipano una possibile apertura cauta delle borse europee e americane.

Il sentiment di mercato è decisamente risk-off, come evidenziato dal calo dei mercati azionari asiatici e dall'aumento della domanda di asset rifugio. L'S&P 500 ha chiuso con un guadagno del 0.54 percento a 6591.90, mentre il VIX è sceso del 6.01 percento a 25.33. Il Dollar Index (DXY) è salito dello 0.07 percento a 99.67. I tassi di interesse continuano a scendere, con il rendimento del decennale USA che ha toccato il 4.32 percento, in calo dell'1.46 percento.

La situazione geopolitica rimane tesa, con l'attenzione focalizzata sul conflitto in corso e le sue implicazioni per i prezzi delle materie prime. L'Opek potrebbe essere influenzata dalle dinamiche del mercato petrolifero.

Sul fronte macroeconomico, gli ultimi dati disponibili non sono recentissimi, ma segnalano una tendenza di rallentamento dell'economia. Il tasso di disoccupazione USA si è attestato al 4.4 percento, mentre il Core CPI è risultato al 2.7 percento. La BCE ha lasciato intendere che potrebbe alzare i tassi in qualsiasi riunione, secondo le parole di Lagarde.

Il mercato delle criptovalute è in calo, con Bitcoin che ha perso lo 0.70 percento e si è attestato a $70,026. L'indice di Fear & Greed è sceso a 10, in territorio di Extreme Fear.

In attesa della ripresa degli scambi, l'attenzione sarà rivolta ai dati macroeconomici in arrivo, come il PIL QoQ USA e la prossima uscita dell'indice dei prezzi al consumo dell'Eurozona. Sarà importante monitorare anche le mosse delle banche centrali e l'evoluzione della situazione geopolitica.

Passiamo ora al comparto degli asset digitali, con un'analisi approfondita delle criptovalute.

Iniziamo con il Bitcoin. Dal punto di vista tecnico, il prezzo attuale è di $70,020, con un calo dell'1.41% rispetto alla giornata precedente. Sul fronte degli ETF, abbiamo registrato un deflusso di $-74.5M, secondo i dati di mercato. Questo dato negativo potrebbe influenzare il sentiment degli investitori nel breve termine.

Passando alle altcoin, Ethereum è scambiata a $2,119, con un calo del 2.08%. Solana e Binance Coin (BNB) seguono la tendenza ribassista, con cali rispettivamente del 3.45% e dell'1.40%, arrivando a $89.13 e $634.92. Questi movimenti potrebbero essere influenzati dalle dinamiche di mercato più ampie e dalle notizie specifiche dei singoli progetti.

L'Indice di Paura ed Avidità si trova a 10, in territorio di Estrema Paura. Questo livello suggerisce che gli investitori sono estremamente cauti, il che potrebbe essere un segnale di un possibile punto di svolta nel mercato. La correlazione con i dati macroeconomici, come il recente tasso di inflazione e i dati sul mercato del lavoro, potrebbe influenzare ulteriormente il sentiment.

In sintesi, il mercato delle criptovalute sembra essere in una fase di consolidamento, con movimenti laterali o ribassisti per le principali criptovalute. Gli investitori continuano a monitorare i dati macroeconomici e le notizie specifiche del settore per orientare le loro strategie.

Grazie per l'attenzione e a domani
audio_script_en:
Welcome to your daily morning market briefing. Good morning and welcome to today's financial update.

Global sentiment is cautious, with investors monitoring ongoing geopolitical tensions. Key data points include the US GDP growth rate, which is yet to be released, and the Eurozone's upcoming CPI data. The current risk-off mood is reflected in the market movements.

In traditional markets, equities are seeing a mixed performance. The S&P 500 is up 0.54% at 6591.90, while the STOXX 600 is up 1.69% at 58.29. The NIKKEI has declined by 0.81% to 53,315. In the currency space, the EUR/USD pair is down 0.45% to 1.15. The Dollar Index is up 0.07% to 99.67. Commodities are under pressure, with Brent crude down 3.04% to $99.11 and Gold down 2.77% to $4423.90 per ounce. Bond yields are declining, with the US 10Y yield at 4.32%, down 1.46%, and the TLT Bond USA 20Y up 0.96% to 86.84.

Geopolitical tensions continue to escalate, with the Iran war having a significant impact on energy prices and global economic growth. The European Central Bank is keeping a close eye on the situation, with ECB President Lagarde stating that they are ready to raise rates at any meeting. The Bank of England is expected to hold off on rate hikes this year. China's oil demand is expected to approach its peak this year, according to CNOOC economist. The global M2 liquidity continues to expand, up 4.9% YoY to $22.7T.

Moving on to the digital assets space, let's take a look at the current state of the cryptocurrency market. 

Starting with Bitcoin, we see that the BTC ETF daily net inflow was $-74.5M, and the price is currently trading at $70,020, down 1.41% from the previous day. From a technical analysis perspective, Bitcoin's recent price action has been characterized by significant outflows from ETFs, which have likely contributed to the recent price decline.

Let's pivot to the cryptocurrency markets and examine some of the top altcoins. Ethereum is trading at $2,119, down 2.08%, while Solana is at $89.13, down 3.45%. Binance Coin is also in the red, trading at $634.92, down 1.40%. 

In terms of sentiment and fear/greed, the Fear & Greed Index is currently at 10, indicating extreme fear. This is often a contrarian indicator, suggesting that investors may be overly pessimistic about the market's prospects. Historically, periods of extreme fear have been followed by market rebounds. 

The cryptocurrency market is highly correlated with broader market trends, and recent economic data releases have been mixed. The PCE Core YoY and Core CPI YoY from the US have shown signs of inflationary pressures, which could impact the market's trajectory. 

In other news, there are growing discussions around the role of Bitcoin as a safe-haven asset, particularly in light of recent global events. Additionally, traditional financial institutions are taking steps to integrate cryptocurrencies into their offerings, with Franklin Templeton and Ondo planning to launch tokenized ETFs with 24/7 trading via crypto wallets.

Thanks for tuning in. We'll be back tomorrow with another market update. Until then, stay informed and stay ahead. A quick look at what's on the radar for tomorrow and we'll wrap up here. Have a great day and we'll catch you soon.

articles.title: Lessons from the 2023 banking turmoil - Executive Summary
articles.snippet: The banking turmoil that started in March 2023 was the most significant period of banking stress since the Great Financial Crisis that began in 2007. Credit Suisse, a Swiss global systemically important bank, along with three regional banks in the United States, with total assets exceeding USD 1 trillion, were either shut down, put into receivership or rescued. These failures triggered a crisis of confidence in banks' resilience and led to wide-scale public support measures.

articles.title: How Are Iran’s Partnerships with Belarus and Russia Holding Up During War?
articles.snippet: In 2025, Jack Roush wrote, “Russia’s War in Ukraine Has Brought Iran and Belarus Closer Together,” where he argued that a closer security partnership between Belarus and Iran could have devastating consequences for Ukraine. Now that Iran is involved in a war with the United States and Israel, we asked Jack to revisit his arguments.Image: khamenei.ir via Wikimedia CommonsIn your 2025 article, you argued that if the security partnership between Belarus and Iran deepens, it could provide a faster, more secure flow of critical drone technology to Russian forces in Ukraine. After another year of war in Ukraine, has this ultimately The post How Are Iran’s Partnerships with Belarus and Russia Holding Up During War? appeared first on War on the Rocks .

articles.title: Proxy Pressure on Iran: The Promise and Pitfalls of Arming the Kurds
articles.snippet: Early in the ongoing war against Iran, the Trump administration and its ally, Israel, believed they could foment a popular uprising to topple the Iranian regime. Part of that plan involved arming Kurdish fighters to infiltrate Iran from their locations in Iraq.The idea had intuitive logic. Kurdish fighters have built a reputation as capable battlefield partners of the U.S. military in recent years, particularly in the campaign against the Islamic State in Syria. Kurdish groups already maintain networks along the Iran–Iraq border and inside Iran. The marriage of their reputation and experience with U.S. capabilities, such as weapons, funding, training, The post Proxy Pressure on Iran: The Promise and Pitfalls of Arming the Kurds appeared first on War on the Rocks .

articles.title: Gas Crisis Dwarfs Oil Shock as LNG Supply Breaks
articles.snippet: Until less than a month ago, most energy analysts were warning about a glut in oil and a likely glut in LNG as new capacity came online in the United States. Glut was the word that described global energy market fundamentals. Then the U.S. and Israeli strikes on Iran on February 28 changed everything, and now everyone is warning of shortages. Oil shortages are already emerging in some parts of the world, but the bigger and worse shortage is in natural gas. QatarEnergy yesterday declared force majeure on LNG contracts with buyers in Italy, China,…
articles.title_it: La crisi del gas naturale eclissa lo shock petrolifero
articles.title_en: Gas Crisis Dwarfs Oil Shock as LNG Supply Breaks
articles.summary_it: La crisi del gas naturale potrebbe avere un impatto maggiore sullo shock petrolifero.
articles.summary_en: The natural gas crisis may have a greater impact than the oil shock.

articles.title: China's Top Shipper Resumes Middle East Trips Amid Iran Ceasefire Talks
articles.snippet: COSCO Shipping Lines, China's largest shipping company, has officially resumed new booking services for container shipments from the Far East to several key Middle Eastern destinations, with the United States and Iran currently engaged in ceasefire talks, despite statements by Tehran that the U.S. military is "negotiating with itself”. COSCO has reinstated bookings for standard general cargo containers to Saudi Arabia, United Arab Emirates, Bahrain, Qatar, Kuwait and Iraq after declaring to the International Maritime Organization (IMO)…

articles.title: Crude Drops 4% on Iran News Just as Dallas Fed Signals Oil Rebound
articles.snippet: A sharp rebound in U.S. oilfield activity is colliding with a sudden geopolitical shock, as the latest Dallas Fed Energy Survey shows energy firms swinging back into expansion just as crude prices plunge roughly 4% on headlines that President Donald Trump has floated a 15-point Iran peace plan, abruptly stripping out part of the war-driven risk premium that had lifted prices more than 30% since late February.Brent crude for May delivery fell 3.9% to trade at $100.40 per barrel at 11.20 am ET while the corresponding WTI crude contract declined 3.77%…

articles.title: Will the Iran War Revive Russia’s Power of Siberia 2 Pipeline?
articles.snippet: The war in Iran and the resulting energy shock could revive China’s interest in a long-stalled pipeline to import Russian gas, analysts told RFE/RL, potentially reshaping Beijing’s energy strategy. As countries are facing an energy shock after Iran shut the Strait of Hormuz, halting oil and liquefied natural gas (LNG) flows from the Gulf, Beijing is grappling with the potential loss of discounted Iranian oil and the risk of prolonged market disruption, prompting a rethink of its reliance on a chokepoint that carries roughly 40 percent…

articles.title: Beyond Oil: The Global Supply Chains Broken by the Iran Conflict
articles.snippet: Since the start of the Middle Eastern conflict nearly a month ago, analysts have kept their eyes glued to the latest energy market data. On 2 March, Iran declared the Strait of Hormuz, a waterway which is responsible for shipping roughly one fifth of the world’s oil and gas, was “closed” and oil prices have slipped out of control ever since. Oil prices surged to over $110 (£82.14) per barrel, leading the US to launch an operation to reopen the strait, deploying jets and helicopters to the region. But while the market tracks…

articles.title: Banks Took $434 Billion From Americans Last Year — Is it Time for Bitcoin?
articles.snippet: Bitcoin Magazine Banks Took $434 Billion From Americans Last Year — Is it Time for Bitcoin? Banks generated $434 billion in 2025 from deposit spreads, while savers lost purchasing power to inflation. Bitcoin shows another way. This post Banks Took $434 Billion From Americans Last Year — Is it Time for Bitcoin? first appeared on Bitcoin Magazine and is written by Micah Zimmerman .

articles.snippet: Available initially to non-US investors, the funds will offer onchain exposure to equities, bonds and gold through crypto wallets.
articles.title_it: Franklin Templeton, Ondo to launch tokenized ETFs with 24/7 trading via crypto wallets
articles.title_en: Franklin Templeton, Ondo to launch tokenized ETFs with 24/7 trading via crypto wallets
